question_relevance_notes.linguistic: Judge asked for text/signs, squarely in linguistic domain.
question_relevance_notes.landscape: Agent was not questioned; default score applied.
question_relevance_notes.botanics: Agent was not questioned; default score applied.
question_relevance_notes.regulatory: Judge asked about license plate details, squarely in regulatory domain.
question_relevance_notes.meta: Judge asked about utility pole/street light design, squarely in meta domain.

response_update_notes.linguistic: Agent responded but only confirmed absence of text; no new positive evidence.
response_update_notes.landscape: Agent was not questioned; default score applied.
response_update_notes.botanics: Agent was not questioned; default score applied.
response_update_notes.regulatory: Agent provided specific new observations (blue strip absence, red strip absence) directly addressing the judge's questions.
response_update_notes.meta: Agent provided specific new observations (swan-neck bracket style) directly addressing the judge's questions.

verdict.judge_question_strategy_notes: The judge correctly identified that the linguistic and regulatory domains held the most potential for differentiation (text and license plates). The questions were targeted and relevant. However, the strategy was slightly imprecise in the regulatory line of questioning, as the license plates in the image are too low-resolution to reliably distinguish between the subtle plate variations (blue/red strips) described, leading to some circularity in the later rounds.
verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge successfully synthesized the meta and regulatory cues to arrive at the correct country (Tunisia). The reasoning was sound, although it relied on the agents' somewhat speculative identification of the 'swan-neck' brackets and plate details.
verdict.discussion_convergence_notes: The discussion showed partial convergence. While the candidates remained similar (Tunisia, Morocco, Algeria) throughout, the specific evidence (plate strips, pole styles) was refined. The process did not eliminate candidates aggressively, but it did narrow the reasoning to specific visual markers.
